channel.title: Frontion News - Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: China economic growth stalls as output and retail sales miss forecasts
item.description: Industrial output and retail sales sharply undershot expectations in April as the world second-biggest economy wrestled with higher energy costs from the Iran war and persistently weak domestic demand.

item.title: Oil prices climb after drone attack on UAE nuclear plant
item.description: Oil prices extended gains as efforts to end the US-Iran conflict appeared stalled following a drone attack on a UAE nuclear power plant and President Trump prepared to discuss military options on Iran.

item.title: German prosecutors expose Russian sanctions-busting network supplying military tech
item.description: A four-year investigation uncovered a Lübeck-based trading firm that routed dual-use components through third countries to Russia, bypassing EU sanctions.

item.title: Brent crude breaks $111 as Gulf drone attacks and Hormuz fears spark shortage concerns
item.description: Oil prices surged in early Asian trading after drone strikes on UAE and Saudi Arabia, combined with ongoing Hormuz disruptions, raised fears of global supply shortages.

item.title: Xi and Trump agree Iran must not obtain nuclear weapons in strategic shift on Gulf security
item.description: China and the US reached a rare point of agreement on Iran policy during Trumps China visit, with both leaders affirming that a nuclear-armed Iran is unacceptable.

item.title: UAE Blames Iran for Drone Strike Near Barakah Nuclear Power Plant
item.description: The United Arab Emirates accused Iran or its proxies of launching a drone that sparked a fire just outside the Barakah nuclear power plant.

item.title: NATO Defense Ministers Meet Without US Leadership Amid Questions
item.description: European allies downplayed concerns after US Defense Secretary Hegseth skipped the NATO gathering in Brussels, as questions about US commitment to the alliance persist.

item.title: G7 Finance Chiefs Convene in Paris to Tackle Trade Imbalances
item.description: Finance ministers from G7 nations meet in Paris to coordinate critical raw material supplies and address global economic tensions as geopolitical differences test the group cohesion.

item.title: Drone Strike Hits UAE Barakah Nuclear Plant Amid Iran War Deadlock
item.description: A drone strike caused a fire at the UAE sole nuclear power plant, with Emirati officials investigating the source and warning of the right to respond to such terrorist attacks.

item.title: Israel to Establish Defense Offices in Former UNRWA East Jerusalem Compound
item.description: Israel plans to set up defense offices in the former UNRWA compound in East Jerusalem after demolishing structures, a move the UN called a violation of international law.

item.title: Russia Destroyed Over 3,000 Ukrainian Drones in Past Week
item.description: Russian defense ministry reports intercepting and destroying at least 3,124 Ukrainian drones over the past week as the air war intensifies.

item.title: UAE Pivots from Oil to AI as Post-War Economic Strategy
item.description: With its OPEC era over, the UAE is betting big on artificial intelligence to build economic bridges with the Global South and diversify away from hydrocarbons.

item.title: Kremlin Signals Readiness to Resume Dialogue with Europe on Ukraine War
item.description: Kremlin spokesperson Dmitry Peskov said Moscow is open to restarting dialogue with Europe over the war in Ukraine following a major Ukrainian drone attack on Moscow.

item.title: Philippine VP Sara Duterte Faces Senate Impeachment Trial
item.description: Philippine Vice President Sara Duterte faces a senate impeachment trial on charges including bribery and corruption, marking a major political crisis.

item.title: G7 Finance Ministers Meet in Paris Amid Trade Tensions and Iran War Fallout
item.description: Finance ministers from the Group of Seven nations convene in Paris to address global economic imbalances and coordinate critical raw material supplies.

item.title: German Finance Minister Calls G7 the Right Forum to End Iran Conflict
item.description: German Finance Minister Lars Klingbeil says the G7 is the appropriate platform to discuss ending the Iran conflict as diplomatic efforts continue.

item.title: CDC Aiding Withdrawal of Americans Potentially Exposed to Ebola in Congo
item.description: The US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention is helping withdraw Americans who may have been exposed to Ebola during the outbreak in Congo.

item.title: Washington Post: CDC Readies Team to Respond to Ebola Outbreak
item.description: The CDC is preparing a response team as the WHO declares the Ebola outbreak in Congo and Uganda a public health emergency of international concern.

item.title: Taiwan President responds to Trump summit: We will not be sacrificed
item.description: Taiwan President Lai Ching-te pushed back after Trump raised doubts about US arms sales and willingness to defend the island, saying Taiwan will not be traded.

item.title: Senate parliamentarian blocks $1 billion White House security funding
item.description: The Senate parliamentarian ruled that Republican efforts to allocate funding for Trump proposed White House ballroom renovation violated budget rules.

item.title: Political executions surge in Iran since war began, UN verifies at least 32
item.description: The United Nations has verified the execution of at least 32 political prisoners in Iran since the US-Israeli military campaign began, raising human rights concerns.

item.title: IDF Extends Death Penalty Law to West Bank Palestinians Convicted of Terror
item.description: Israel Defense Forces Central Command chief has applied a new death penalty law to Palestinians in the West Bank convicted of terrorism offenses.

item.title: Argentina Retires A-4 Fightinghawks After Six Decades
item.description: Argentina has finally stood down its A-4 Skyhawks, the type first export operator, after 60 years of service as the air force modernizes.

item.title: Norway Arrests Chinese Man for Spying
item.description: Norway interior security service has arrested a Chinese national on suspicion of espionage, marking a rare public espionage case in the Nordic country.

item.title: Ukraine Drone Attack Kills Four in Moscow Biggest Strike in Over a Year
item.description: At least four people were killed after Ukraine launched its largest drone attack on Moscow in more than a year, striking residential areas.
